preamble: The transformation from a voiced labio-velar approximant ([w]) to a voiceless bilabial plosive ([p]) is a type of fortition, where a sound becomes 'stronger' by increasing its degree of stricture. This shift also involves devoicing, as the voiced approximant becomes a voiceless plosive. The change from labio-velar to bilabial indicates a simplification of the place of articulation to a purely labial one.
phoneticEffects: Fortition, Devoicing, Labialization (reduction of velar component)

examples.note: In the morphophonemic alternation from the stem 'kiruwl-' to the imperative form 'kiruph', the /w/ fortifies to /p/.

sources: Robinett, F. (1955). Hidatsa I: Morphophonemics. International Journal of American Linguistics, 21(1), 1-7.
Park, I. (2012). A Grammar of Hidatsa. Indiana University.
